title: Macrame shelf
subtitle: A knotted cotton cord shelf hanger using square knots to support a 40 cm wooden shelf plank
excerpt: A macrame shelf hanger suspends a wooden plank from the wall using knotted cotton cord on two dowels: one for mounting at the top and one threaded through the lower section to keep the shelf level. Square knots on both sides of the shelf hold the plank in position. This hanger fits a 40 x 10 cm plank and hangs approximately 80 cm from the top dowel to the bottom fringe.
sourceNotes: Macrame shelf hangers are a standard contemporary macrame project. Method is observation-led and consistent with current macrame instruction.

content.text: on each side of the shelf plank, anchored top and bottom by horizontal dowels. The
below the lower dowel is a decorative finish and not structural.

items.name: Wooden dowel, 50 cm (for shelf support below plank)
Wooden shelf plank, 40 x 10 cm, 1-2 cm thick
Drill with 6 mm bit (to drill cord holes in shelf plank)

content.text: Drill two 6 mm holes in the shelf plank, one at each end, 2 cm from the front edge. These holes allow two cords from each side to pass through and be knotted below the shelf to support it. Sand any rough edges around the holes.
Step 2: Mount cords and work the upper columns

Cut 8 cords, each 10 m long. Fold each in half and mount 4 on the left end of the top dowel and 4 on the right end using lark's head knots (16 working cords on each side, 8 per side). Work a column of 15-18 square knots on each group of 4 cords on each side of the dowel: 4 separate columns total. These columns become the upper suspension cords above the shelf.
Step 3: Thread the shelf and second dowel

Thread 2 cords from each side group through the drilled holes in the shelf plank from above. Slide the shelf up the cords to the desired height. Tie a square knot directly below the shelf on each side to hold it in position. Then gather all cords from each side and thread them around the second dowel, securing with lark's head knots or by knotting tightly above the dowel.
Step 4: Knot the lower section and fringe

Below the second dowel, work a short section of square knots on each side (5-6 knots per column) to create a lower decorative section. Leave the remaining cord as fringe. Trim the fringe to an even length or cut at a slight angle. Tie a loop of linen cord to each end of the top dowel for hanging.

items.fix: Count square knots carefully: each side must have the same number. Check the shelf level with a spirit level before knotting the shelf into its final position.
items.cause: Upper columns on one side worked to a different length than the other
items.problem: The shelf tilts to one side when loaded

items.fix: Tie a square knot or overhand knot on each cord immediately below the shelf plank before sliding the shelf to its position. The knot prevents the cord pulling upward through the hole.
items.cause: No square knot or stop knot tied directly below each hole
items.problem: The cords below the shelf pull out through the drilled holes

items.fix: Hold the two filler cords taut and parallel while tying each square knot. If they go slack, the knot will not pack against the previous one evenly.
items.cause: Inconsistent tension on the filler cords during knotting
items.problem: The columns are uneven in width with visible loose sections
